shape_decode: cmp=L, vp=W, vpc=_, naive=L : value_profile resolves; cmplog AND naive block; value_profile_cmplog non-decisive => a BARE VP CMP_MAP operand-distance gradient that I2S substitution (cmplog) AND blind havoc (naive) both fail to climb.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (cmp,vp,vpc,naive)=(L,W,_,L). The only technique present in the winner (value_profile) and absent in the decisive losers (naive; cmplog which carries I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback. The Layer-1 attribution is 'bare VP gradient'; THIS test is the independent real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient manifests in the corpus.
round_history: round0: whole shape on value_distance_reached (literal + numeric); 4/7 validated (short-operand chance-flagged), 3 inconclusive. round2: pivot to joint_necessity token+size (H1) + operand_enrichment (H2); the 4 sqlite3 operator branches + lcms_2141 labeled, but H1 did NOT hold for the 3 STRUCTURAL/THRESHOLD branches -- libxml2_6756 (CDATA opener), sqlite3_14777 (token-CLASS GetToken), lcms_2446 (numeric threshold) all rule_not_met / decidable:false. round3 adds GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanisms using BUILT descriptors NOT yet tried in this shape: depth_reach (parser-depth into the structured true-side, for the token-class / structural-entry gates that carry no single literal nor a counted token-richness signal) and value_distance_reached NUMERIC mode (now BUILT/validated per registry) for the magnitude-threshold gate.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient drives corpus admission toward structurally larger / more-token-rich seeds: the VP-RESOLVING corpus physically CONTAINS more of the gate's required structural tokens (SQL keyword/operator tokens; the '<![CDATA[' opener literal) than the naive-BLOCKING corpus, because the gradient rewards partial assembly that edge-only feedback discards. STRUCTURAL-ASSEMBLY / token-availability advantage, NOT scalar value-distance.

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=naive). LW_L has NO value_profile BLOCKING seeds (value_profile RESOLVES) and the DB carries NO cmplog BLOCKING seeds for these branches -- the only blocking arm with seeds is naive. The discriminating contrast is W=value_profile-resolving vs L=naive-blocking, emitted as winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift + winner_size/loser_size/size_lift.

tokens_by_target.sqlite3_14438: SQL structural tokens incl. the '>>' shift operator: --tokens 'SELECT,FROM,WHERE,>>,>=,<=,=,<<,-,/,*'.
tokens_by_target.sqlite3_15515: SQL tokens incl. the two-char operator second-char '=': --tokens 'SELECT,FROM,WHERE,>=,<=,=,-,/,*'.

hypotheses.prediction: tag_lift = winner_tags - loser_tags >= 1.0 (the value_profile-resolving seed set carries at least one MORE of the gate's required structural tokens than the naive-blocking set). DISCRIMINATING vs round-0 scalar-distance: tag_lift counts whole-token PRESENCE, orthogonal to residual Hamming distance to one operand. DISCRIMINATING vs a naive-found-it-anyway alternative: blind havoc would carry the tokens too (loser_tags ~ winner_tags, tag_lift ~ 0). DISCRIMINATING vs pure-I2S-substitution: cmplog carries the identical I2S mutator yet still BLOCKS (Layer-1), so single-literal substitution does not place these multi-token structures. EMPIRICAL ANCHOR (s4, value_profile vs naive): sqlite3_14438 tag_lift 3.5, 15242 tag_lift 3.8, 15515 tag_lift 2.73.
hypotheses.rule_note: tag_lift >= 1.0 = value_profile corpus carries at least one more structural token than naive (confirmed 2.7-3.8 on the 3 retained sqlite3 operator branches). size_lift >= 1.5 is the backstop. OR not AND. Both over named metrics only. NOTE: membership narrowed from round-2 -- sqlite3_14777 (token-CLASS, no counted operator literal) and libxml2_6756 (structural-entry, opener built not counted) DID NOT hold under tag_lift in round-2 and are MOVED OUT to the depth_reach hypothesis below; the 3 sqlite3 operator branches that DID confirm are retained.

hypotheses.label: for the fixed-offset FOURCC gate (lcms_2141 DeviceClass 'scnr' at offset 0x0c-0x0f, decoy 'mntr'), value_profile's gradient enriches the resolving corpus for the TARGET operand bytes relative to the DECOY bytes at the gate offset, because near-miss FOURCC bytes register as new CMP_MAP buckets and steer the corpus toward 'scnr'.

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_branch(winner_fuzzer=value_profile, loser_fuzzer=naive). lcms_2141 is the ONE fixed-offset literal-vs-decoy gate in this shape: W seeds carry 'scnr' (73 63 6e 72) at 0x0c-0x0f, L seeds carry 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at the SAME offset; gate_signature auto-derives the per-offset (target,decoy) pair, then enrichment = log2 of target-byte freq in the value_profile corpus vs the naive corpus. (sB-local corpus; sB runs operand_enrichment on its own on-disk lcms queue -- NOT decidable:false.)

params.scope: lcms_2141 ONLY. The other branches are dispersed/variable-offset operators, token-class structures, or numeric thresholds -- operand_enrichment correctly returns no_gate_signature for them, so byte_freq_ratio is applied to lcms_2141 alone. data_realities #3 honored.
params.caveat: 4-byte FOURCC operand (width=4) -- above the width<=2 chance-alignment band, and target vs decoy are DISTINCT 4-byte values, so the target_vs_decoy contrast is clean. agg=max recovers the single most-differentiating offset.

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0 AND signed_target_enrich > decoy_enrich: the value_profile-resolving corpus is enriched for 'scnr' target bytes relative to 'mntr' decoy bytes at offset 0x0c-0x0f. DISCRIMINATING vs naive luck: a no-gradient corpus shows signed_target_enrich ~ 0. DISCRIMINATING vs pure-I2S: cmplog (I2S, no gradient) still BLOCKS (Layer-1), so on-demand substitution does not persistently enrich the corpus; the gradient does. RULED OUT: chance co-occurrence -> signed_target_enrich would not exceed decoy_enrich.
hypotheses.rule_note: standard operand_enrichment pro-signature (matches the tool's validated pro median +3.12). Both clauses over the tool's named metrics only.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round3): for the token-CLASS / structural-ENTRY gates that have NO single counted literal -- sqlite3_14777 (GetToken classifies a char into the token class whose true branch fires) and libxml2_6756 (xmlParseCDSect entry reaches the !IS_CHAR true side) -- value_profile's gradient admits a corpus that PARSES DEEPER: its resolving seeds drive the gate line's TRUE side substantially more per-seed than the naive-blocking seeds, because the gradient preserves structurally-valid partial parses that reach the comparison context, whereas naive's edge-only seeds are byte-soup that never enters the structured branch. EXECUTION-DEPTH / true-side reach advantage, NOT token byte-count (which round-2's tag_lift could not capture for these two: 14777 is a token-CLASS with no single operator literal to count; 6756's opener is assembled, not present as a counted XML structural token).

measurement.tool_mode: Run the value_profile-RESOLVING seeds (W) and the naive-BLOCKING seeds (L) for each branch through the libafl-<target>-cov image; read the gate line's per-seed-normalized execution count + true/blocked-side count from llvm-cov. depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth (per-seed hit normalization), side_lift = winner true-side count vs loser true-side count. The gate is the GetToken token-class true branch (sqlite3.c:159496) / the xmlParseCDSect !IS_CHAR(s) line (parser.c:9890). sqlite3 has an on-disk cov image on s4; libxml2 is sB's target and sB owns its libafl-libxml2-cov image -- NOT decidable:false for being off THIS server.

params.side: true (W marks the true side; L blocks it -- L T=0 in both cards)

hypotheses.prediction: winner_deeper = true AND side_lift >= 2.0: the value_profile-resolving seeds reach the gate's TRUE side markedly more than the naive-blocking seeds, which (per both cards) hit T=0 on the gate line -- so the true-side reach is uniquely the VP corpus's. DISCRIMINATING vs round-2 token-presence (tag_lift): depth_reach measures ACTUAL execution into the structured branch, which is exactly the signal that exists when there is no countable literal token (14777 token-class; 6756 assembled opener) -- a gate can fire its true side without any one fixed operator literal being present to count, so depth_lift>0 with tag_lift~0 is the precise round-2 failure mode this resolves. DISCRIMINATING vs a naive-reaches-it-anyway alternative: if blind havoc parsed into the structured branch, loser true-side count would be non-trivial (side_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper false) -- the cards' L T=0 predicts the opposite. DISCRIMINATING vs pure-I2S: cmplog carries I2S yet BLOCKS (Layer-1), so single-literal substitution does not drive the parser into these multi-condition structured contexts; only the gradient-admitted structured corpus reaches the true side.
hypotheses.rule_note: winner_deeper is depth_reach's validated boolean (winner corpus reaches the line deeper/more per-seed); side_lift >= 2.0 = value_profile reaches the gate TRUE side at least 2x the naive rate -- with both cards reporting naive true-side T=0, any non-trivial VP true-side reach clears this. Both over depth_reach's named metrics only. depth_reach is BLIND to ctx/ngram context-reach subtypes, but these gates are line-reach (not per-call-context) gates, so the standard cov binary suffices.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round3): lcms_2446 (TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG, inequality_or_range, derived_integer u32) -- value_profile's gradient drives the parsed numeric TagCount field MONOTONICALLY toward / across the MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold via CMP_MAP operand-distance buckets on the integer comparison, while naive (edge-only) and cmplog (no single constant to splice for an inequality) stall below the bound. MAGNITUDE-GAP gradient (no literal, no countable token), measured by value_distance_reached's NUMERIC mode. This SUPERSEDES round-2's decidable:false tool-gap: the registry now lists value_distance_reached with numeric mode (magnitude gap for inequality gates) as BUILT/validated, so the magnitude-threshold gate IS measurable.
replaces: vp_numeric_threshold_no_built_descriptor (decidable:false tool-gap; value_distance_reached numeric mode is now BUILT)

measurement.tool_mode: NUMERIC mode (registry: 'numeric mode = magnitude gap for inequality gates'). For each arm's seeds, parse the derived TagCount field (big-endian u32, same width/endianness as the gate's _cmsReadUInt32Number at cmsio0.c:826) and compute the magnitude gap to the MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold compared at line 827; the winner (value_profile) lands CLOSE to / past the threshold, losers (naive) stall below. Arms with seeds here are value_profile-RESOLVING (W) vs naive-BLOCKING (L); cmplog has no blocking seeds in the DB for this branch. lcms is sB's target; sB runs value_distance_reached on its own on-disk lcms corpus -- NOT decidable:false for being off THIS server.

params.operand_literal: MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold (resolved from the lcms build header); field = TagCount, 4-byte big-endian u32 parsed by _cmsReadUInt32Number at cmsio0.c:826; gate = TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG at line 827. Numeric magnitude-gap mode (NOT per-byte Hamming).
params.caveat: width=4 is above the width<=2 chance-alignment band; numeric mode compares parsed magnitudes, not byte windows, so short-operand chance-alignment does not apply.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap > 0: value_profile-resolving seeds carry TagCount AT or PAST MAX_TABLE_TAG (vp_min_distance ~ 0) while naive-blocking seeds stay below (naive_min_distance > 0), so the value_profile corpus is magnitude-closer to crossing. DISCRIMINATING vs literal substitution: an inequality gate has NO single target constant for I2S to splice -- ANY value above the bound resolves -- so cmplog cannot win by substitution and stays bounded below (Layer-1: cmplog BLOCKS), exactly distinguishing the magnitude-climb from a literal gate (where the discriminator would be per-byte distance to one operand). DISCRIMINATING vs naive luck: if blind havoc had landed a large TagCount, naive_min_distance would be ~0 and winner_closer false -- the card reports naive T=0 F=10 (never crossed), predicting the opposite. A null (vp_min_distance ~ naive_min_distance) refutes the gradient-climb story.
hypotheses.rule_note: value_distance_reached's validated winner_closer boolean + a positive distance_gap (winner magnitude-closer to the threshold than the loser). NUMERIC mode only -- no per-byte literal distance. Replaces the round-2 decidable:false tool-gap now that numeric mode is BUILT.

hypotheses.label: value_profile-resolving seeds reach a multi-byte operator/keyword/structural LITERAL strictly closer than cmplog-blocking AND naive-blocking seeds; the operand is not a single spliceable constant, so I2S stalls and naive gets no gradient while the CMP_MAP distance bucket ratchets a prefix/Hamming approach to the literal

measurement.registry_tool_note: Reuses value_distance_reached (literal mode): for each branch locate the gate operand window in each seed, compute per-seed MIN byte-distance to the target literal, take the median over the value_profile-RESOLVING (vp_dist), cmplog-BLOCKING (cmp_dist), naive-BLOCKING (naive_dist) seed sets. Report lit_dist_gap = min(cmp_dist, naive_dist) - vp_dist and vp_dist_frac = vp_dist / operand_width.

operands_per_branch.lcms_2141: scnr (73 63 6e 72, 4-byte DeviceClass FOURCC at offset 0x0c-0x0f; decoy mntr=6d 6e 74 72)
operands_per_branch.sqlite3_14438: >> (3e 3e, two adjacent shift-operator bytes embedded in a SELECT expression)

params.distance_metric: per_byte_abs_sum_min_over_offsets (contiguous literals); adjacency literals (sqlite3 >>, >=, =-after-operator): unmatched-bytes of best adjacency occurrence

hypotheses.prediction: value_profile-resolving seeds sit CLOSE to the multi-byte literal (vp_dist_frac low) while BOTH cmplog-blocking and naive-blocking seeds sit FAR, so lit_dist_gap > 0 by a clear margin. DISCRIMINATING vs I2S substitution: ordinary single-literal I2S would let cmplog splice the constant (cmp_dist near 0 on some seeds), collapsing lit_dist_gap toward 0. DISCRIMINATING vs naive luck: naive_dist far confirms blind havoc never assembled the literal.
hypotheses.rule: lit_dist_gap >= 1.0 AND vp_dist_frac <= 0.34 AND cmp_dist > vp_dist AND naive_dist > vp_dist
hypotheses.rule_note: lit_dist_gap >= 1.0 = vp at least one full byte-equivalent closer than the nearer loser; vp_dist_frac <= 0.34 = vp within ~1 byte of a multi-byte target on median; strict-ordering clauses enforce vp closer than cmplog AND naive separately. All over named metrics only.
hypotheses.note_restored: round-0 validated >=1 branch; KEEP (superset/monotonicity). Note: libxml2_6756 and sqlite3_14777 are NOT in this hypothesis's operand list -- round-0 found them unmeasurable/refuted under per-byte literal distance; they are carried by the new depth_reach hypothesis.

hypotheses.label: value_profile-resolving seeds drive a derived NUMERIC field past an inequality/range threshold; there is no exact literal to splice, so distance is the MAGNITUDE GAP to crossing the threshold -- vp closes it monotonically while cmplog (no single constant to substitute) and naive (no gradient) stall below the bound

measurement.registry_tool_note: Reuses value_distance_reached NUMERIC mode (magnitude gap for inequality gates): parse the derived numeric field from each seed (same parser as the gate: _cmsReadUInt32Number big-endian u32 for TagCount) and compute the signed MAGNITUDE GAP to the threshold, clamped at 0 once crossed. Median over value_profile-RESOLVING (vp_gap), cmplog-BLOCKING (cmp_gap), naive-BLOCKING (naive_gap). mag_gap_lift = min(cmp_gap, naive_gap) - vp_gap; vp_crossed_frac = fraction of vp-resolving seeds with field_value > threshold.

operands_per_branch.lcms_2446: TagCount vs MAX_TABLE_TAG (4-byte big-endian u32 parsed at cmsio0.c:826, compared > MAX_TABLE_TAG at line 827; gap = MAX_TABLE_TAG + 1 - TagCount, clamped at 0 once crossed)

hypotheses.prediction: value_profile-resolving seeds carry TagCount AT or ABOVE the threshold (vp_crossed_frac high, vp_gap ~ 0) while cmplog-blocking and naive-blocking seeds stay below (cmp_gap, naive_gap > 0), so mag_gap_lift > 0. DISCRIMINATING vs literal substitution: a threshold gate has no single constant for I2S to splice -- ANY value above the bound resolves -- so cmplog cannot win by substitution and stays bounded below.
hypotheses.rule: vp_crossed_frac >= 0.5 AND mag_gap_lift > 0 AND cmp_gap > vp_gap AND naive_gap > vp_gap
hypotheses.rule_note: vp_crossed_frac >= 0.5 = a median vp-resolving seed crosses the threshold; mag_gap_lift > 0 with strict-ordering = vp magnitude-closer than cmplog AND naive. Magnitude-gap metric only.

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator (data_realities #1,#2) -> measured ONLY via what the corpus REACHES: structural token count + size (joint_necessity), fixed-offset operand byte enrichment (operand_enrichment), per-seed branch-line execution depth (depth_reach), and parsed-value magnitude distance (value_distance_reached numeric). NO seed_lineage 'gradient event'.
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence proposed.

joint/structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds (data_realities #3) -> byte_freq_ratio (fixed-offset alignment) applied ONLY to lcms_2141; the dispersed/token-class/structural-entry gates use token counting, per-seed branch-line depth (no offset alignment needed), or parsed-numeric magnitude.

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 (data_realities #4) -> the sqlite3 branches are scored on s4 from its on-disk corpus + DB seeds + libafl-sqlite3-cov image. lcms_2141, lcms_2446, libxml2_6756 are sB's targets: sB scores them from its OWN on-disk corpus + db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps carry resolving+blocking seeds, data_realities #5) + its libafl-{lcms,libxml2}-cov images. NOT marked decidable:false merely for being off THIS server.

depth_reach is BLIND to the ctx/ngram per-call-context reach subtype (data_realities / registry scope_limit), but sqlite3_14777 (GetToken line true side) and libxml2_6756 (xmlParseCDSect line true side) are LINE-reach gates measurable by the standard cov binary -- not per-call-context gates -- so depth_reach applies cleanly.

LW_L has NO value_profile BLOCKING arm and the DB carries NO cmplog BLOCKING seeds -> joint_necessity, depth_reach, and value_distance_reached are all used in winner(value_profile) vs loser(naive) mode; cmplog-arm metrics (cmp_dist/cmp_gap) appear only in the two KEEP value_distance_reached hypotheses where the metric is defined as min over available blocking arms (naive present, cmplog absent -> degenerates to naive, still strict-ordering-valid).
notes:
ROUND 3 (loop-until-dry). The 3 still-unlabeled branches after round-2 each needed a GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism, because round-2's joint_necessity tag_lift (token byte-count) structurally cannot fire for them:

PRIORS KEPT (superset / monotonic, unchanged so confirmed branches re-validate):
  - vp_admits_token_richer_structural_corpus (KEEP; joint_necessity) -- confirmed on the sqlite3 OPERATOR branches. Membership NARROWED to the 3 that actually held under tag_lift (14438/15242/15515); 14777 and 6756 (which round-2 also listed but which did NOT hold) are MOVED to the new depth_reach hypothesis. The KEEP hypothesis + its rule are unchanged; only the membership list shrinks to the confirmers, which is monotone-safe (it still re-validates the confirmed branches and stops mislabeling the two it could not support).
  - vp_enriches_fixed_offset_fourcc_operand (KEEP; operand_enrichment) -- lcms_2141, unchanged.
  - vp_gradient_assembles_multibyte_literal (KEEP; value_distance_reached literal) -- round-0 confirmer, unchanged.
  - vp_gradient_climbs_numeric_threshold (KEEP; value_distance_reached numeric) -- round-0 confirmer, unchanged.

NEW MECHANISM(S) this round:
  (1) vp_drives_parser_depth_into_structured_true_side (decidable; descriptor seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch, registry_tool=depth_reach -- UNTRIED in this shape). For sqlite3_14777 (token-CLASS GetToken true branch) and libxml2_6756 (xmlParseCDSect !IS_CHAR true side): these gates fire on a structured PARSE reaching a comparison context, with NO single operator literal to count -- so tag_lift could not separate them (round-2 rule_not_met). depth_reach measures per-seed execution into the gate's TRUE side; both cards report the naive/loser true-side at T=0, so any non-trivial VP true-side reach yields winner_deeper + side_lift>=2. This is orthogonal to token byte-count and to scalar value-distance -- it is execution-depth, the natural signal for token-class / structural-entry gates.
  (2) vp_climbs_numeric_magnitude_to_threshold (decidable; descriptor seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch NUMERIC mode, registry_tool=value_distance_reached). For lcms_2446 (TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG). Round-2 marked this decidable:false on a TOOL-GAP claim ('numeric-magnitude mode unbuilt'), but the registry NOW lists value_distance_reached with 'numeric mode = magnitude gap for inequality gates' as BUILT/validated (anchor sqlite3/14437, /code-review DONE). So the magnitude crossing IS measurable: winner_closer + distance_gap>0 means the VP corpus carries TagCount magnitude-closer to / past the threshold than naive (card: naive T=0 F=10, never crossed). This SUPERSEDES the round-2 decidable:false honest gap, now that the tool exists.

DECIDABLE:FALSE this round: NONE. All 7 branches now carry a decidable hypothesis backed by a BUILT registry tool. (Round-2's only decidable:false, lcms_2446's numeric-threshold tool-gap, is closed by the now-built value_distance_reached numeric mode -- an honest correction, not a forced label: the registry explicitly lists numeric mode as validated.) If sB's arbiter finds depth_reach cannot run on its libxml2 cov image, libxml2_6756 falls to the fallback=inconclusive -- but the test itself is valid for ALL branches and is not pre-marked decidable:false for server location.

DISCRIMINABILITY (G1): the new depth_reach hypothesis predicts winner_deeper=true with naive true-side T=0 (card-grounded); a naive-reaches-it alternative would give side_lift~1; a pure-I2S alternative is ruled out by Layer-1 (cmplog carries I2S yet BLOCKS). The new numeric hypothesis predicts winner_closer with naive stalled below the bound; a substitution alternative is impossible for an inequality gate (no single constant), and naive-luck would give winner_closer=false. G2 INDEPENDENCE: every metric (token count, byte enrichment, per-seed line-depth, parsed-magnitude distance) is a corpus-STATE / execution quantity over the seed sets, computed independently of the value_profile-resolves pattern that defined the shape.

NO NEW DESCRIPTOR: every hypothesis REUSES a BUILT registry tool by descriptor_key (joint_necessity, operand_enrichment, depth_reach, value_distance_reached x2). TARGETS: lcms, libxml2, sqlite3.
